epigraph.text: Democracy is the theory that the common people know what they want, and deserve to get it good and hard.

follyOfTheDay.headline: Yale professor stripped of tenure over Oxford comma usage
follyOfTheDay.summary:
In what can only be described as the apotheosis of academic overreach, a tenured literature professor at Yale University has been relieved of his position following a complaint that his use of the Oxford comma in classroom materials constituted grammatical oppression. The student who filed the grievance claimed that prescriptivist punctuation standards exemplified colonial heteronormativity. The university's administration, apparently seizing the opportunity to demonstrate institutional commitment to linguistic flexibility, voted unanimously to terminate the professor's thirty-year career. The English Department subsequently announced plans to establish a new course: "Deconstructing the Tyranny of Syntax."

This incident represents the logical endpoint of an educational philosophy that has elevated student grievance to the status of institutional law. When a comma becomes a matter of social justice, we have crossed from the realm of academic freedom into pure theater.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: Nothing says commitment to free inquiry like firing your most productive scholar for his preference in punctuation.
